serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve carton of flavoured soy milk is approximately 250 ml (250 g), commonly consumed as a breakfast drink or snack beverage in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Soy Milk — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for flavoured soy milk (packaged), CR ID D02030002. All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Soy milk, flavoured
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 34 (low) for flavoured soy milk is consistent with published GI values for sweetened soy milk in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021). Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: (34 × 8.36 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 250 g / 100) / 100 = 7.1, rounded to 7.0. Input record cited GI 34 with GL 2.8 (likely per 100 g basis); GL recalculated here per standard 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (6.56 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 44 mg/100 g.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this flavoured soy milk due to its sugar-sweetened beverage status (6.56 g sugar/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold), as dietary sugar independently raises blood pressure regardless of the low sodium content (44 mg/100 g); switching to unsweetened soy milk is recommended.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.56 g sugar/100 g and minimal dietary fibre (0.33 g/100 g), resulting in rapid glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This flavoured soy milk is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes because it qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (6.56 g sugar/100 g) with negligible fibre (0.33 g/100 g), causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; unsweetened soy milk is a suitable low-sugar alternative that retains the protein and isoflavone benefits of soy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Soy protein and isoflavones offer cardioprotective benefits, but added sugar (6.56 g/100 g) in beverage form raises CVD risk through triglyceride elevation and inflammatory pathways.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While soy protein (2.49 g/100 g) and isoflavones in this product provide modest cardioprotective effects, the added sugar content (6.56 g/100 g) in liquid form can elevate triglycerides and promote low-grade inflammation, making unsweetened soy milk a preferable choice for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Soy isoflavones provide anti-inflammatory polyphenol activity; calcium (101 mg/100 g) supports bone health relevant to RA patients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Flavoured soy milk is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to soy isoflavone anti-inflammatory properties and calcium content (101 mg/100 g), but the 6.56 g sugar/100 g is borderline and excess sugar may promote inflammatory mediators; the unsweetened variant is preferred.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured soy milk (packaged) is a ready-to-drink plant-based beverage made from soybeans with added flavouring agents and sugar, widely consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is produced by blending soy extract with water, sweeteners, and flavourings, then pasteurising and packaging in single-serve cartons or bottles. The beverage has a mildly sweet, creamy taste with a characteristic soy undertone, and a smooth, thin liquid texture. Per 100 g, it provides 59 kcal, 2.49 g protein, 1.72 g fat, 8.36 g carbohydrate (of which 6.56 g is sugar), 0.33 g dietary fibre, 44 mg sodium, 101 mg calcium, and 0.71 mg iron. The calcium content (101 mg/100 g) is notable, often from added calcium carbonate, making it a useful dairy-free calcium source. Soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) present in soy milk provide polyphenol-based anti-inflammatory and mild oestrogenic activity relevant to bone health and cardiovascular protection. Clinically, this product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its 6.56 g sugar/100 g content, which triggers SSB hard gates for both Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension management guidelines. The glycemic index is low (GI 34), but the liquid form means sugar is absorbed rapidly, worsening postprandial glucose response. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those with lactose intolerance or dairy allergy. The unsweetened variant is strongly preferred for individuals managing diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: Is flavoured soy milk good for diabetics?
Can I drink soy milk if I have high blood pressure?
How much sugar is in flavoured soy milk?
What is the GI of soy milk?
Is flavoured soy milk suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (CR ID D02030002, confidence 0.95). GI value of 34 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database published values for sweetened soy milk. All core macros and key secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this packaged flavoured soy milk is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (D02030002) as of 2026. potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo source record for this product. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: soy-based beverages contain negligible trans fat; confirmed consistent with HPB and USDA data for soy milk products. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0: plant-based soy milk contains no cholesterol; naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.25 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for sweetened soy milk (NDB 14191), consistent with the total fat of 1.72 g/100 g reported by HPB. GI glycemic load recalculated per standard 250 g serving: GL = (34 × 8.36 × 250) / 10000 = 7.1, rounded to 7.0. Input record GL of 2.8 appears to be a per-100 g basis calculation. This product qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and WHO definitions (sugar ≥ 5 g/100 g in a beverage), triggering clinical caution flags for Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension.
